summary: Ride sharing and on-demand delivery platform Uber (NYSE:UBER) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, but sales rose 14.5% year on year to $13.2 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.72 per share was 3.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Uber forecasts strong second-quarter bookings despite Middle East woes
summary: Uber Technologies forecast second-quarter bookings above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday on strong demand for ride-hailing and delivery services, even though the Middle East ‌conflict weighed on its growth.  Uber said it expects gross bookings of $56.25 billion to $57.75 billion for the June ‌quarter, above analysts' average estimate of $56.07 ⁠billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.  It factors in a roughly 60 basis-point drag from the Middle East conflict.

content.title: Uber forecasts strong second-quarter bookings despite Middle East woes
content.summary: Uber Technologies forecast second-quarter bookings above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday on strong demand for ride-hailing and delivery services, even though the Middle East ‌conflict weighed on its growth.  Uber said it expects gross bookings of $56.25 billion to $57.75 billion for the June ‌quarter, above analysts' average estimate of $56.07 ⁠billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.  It factors in a roughly 60 basis-point drag from the Middle East conflict.

summary: DoorDash stock rallied late Wednesday after the food-delivery company reported first-quarter earnings that beat estimates despite revenue slightly below expectations. headline: Uber stock jumps on strong forecast despite Middle East woes
summary:
STORY: Shares of Uber rose eight and a half percent Wednesday after the company said bookings in the second quarter could hit nearly $58 billion, exceeding Wall Street estimates.

It also gave an earnings forecast that was largely above the consensus forecast of analysts, though revenue in the first quarter missed estimates.

Uber is seeing strong demand for its ride-hailing and delivery services even though the Middle East conflict weighed on its growth.

One analyst told Reuters demand is holding up, and Uber can gain market share in the US suburbs and international markets.

In terms of its business overseas, Uber pointed to strong performance in Australia and new markets such as Denmark.

Uber also said its growing adoption of artificial intelligence tools is helping moderate the pace of hiring by improving productivity across its operations.

When it comes to autonomous vehicles, Uber is pursuing a partnership-led approach, working with more than 20 companies to integrate robotaxis onto its platform rather than building the technology itself.

content.title: Uber stock jumps on strong forecast despite Middle East woes
content.summary:
STORY: Shares of Uber rose eight and a half percent Wednesday after the company said bookings in the second quarter could hit nearly $58 billion, exceeding Wall Street estimates.

It also gave an earnings forecast that was largely above the consensus forecast of analysts, though revenue in the first quarter missed estimates.

Uber is seeing strong demand for its ride-hailing and delivery services even though the Middle East conflict weighed on its growth.

One analyst told Reuters demand is holding up, and Uber can gain market share in the US suburbs and international markets.

In terms of its business overseas, Uber pointed to strong performance in Australia and new markets such as Denmark.

Uber also said its growing adoption of artificial intelligence tools is helping moderate the pace of hiring by improving productivity across its operations.

When it comes to autonomous vehicles, Uber is pursuing a partnership-led approach, working with more than 20 companies to integrate robotaxis onto its platform rather than building the technology itself.

headline: Market Minute 5-6-26- Stocks Soar, Oil Tanks on New Talks
summary: Stocks are soaring while oil is tanking amid reports of new Middle East talks. Gold and silver are ripping along with Treasuries, while the dollar is sliding. Bitcoin is trading at fresh three-month highs above $82,000.
